title: Cleome kelleriana
taxonomy: Cleome kelleriana is classified in the family Cleomaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as spider flowers or bee flowers. This family belongs to the order Brassicales, a large and diverse group of plants that also includes mustards, cabbages, and capers. The order Brassicales is further classified under the subclass Dilleniidae, which is a subgroup of flowering plants that also includes the order Malvales. The class Magnoliopsida, to which the plant Cleome kelleriana belongs, includes a wide variety of flowering plants such as roses, magnolias, and orchids. intro: Cleome kelleriana, also known as spider flower, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome kelleriana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Cleome kelleriana is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flowers and root extracts, which are used in traditional medicine and homeopathy.
